name: School Transportation Of Kids
recent_news: No public news identified; not listed on the 2025 Miami-Dade County Public Schools approved private bus companies roster, suggesting a very small private/charter-school shuttle operator.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~9 employees, ~$400K revenue) Miami-area operator owned by Marco Diez. Too small to be a meaningful tuck-in on its own; potentially a route-add if competing with an existing Summit Miami platform, but ownership/fleet not verifiable online.

name: Lga School Transportation Inc
districts_or_customers: Lee County / Lehigh Acres area (likely charter/private school routes)
recent_news: No public news, website or press identified; appears to be a micro-operator alongside peers like Lisdie Montano School Transportation and Boys and Girls Transport in Lehigh Acres.
fit_notes: Very small Lehigh Acres operator (~9 employees, ~$378K revenue). Below typical tuck-in threshold; only interesting as a low-cost route bolt-on if Summit builds a SW Florida hub.

name: Robert's Hawaii School Bus Inc
owner_or_parent: Roberts Hawaii Inc (employee-owned via ESOP, founded 1941 by Robert Iwamoto Sr.)
districts_or_customers: Hawaii State Department of Education (largest Oahu school bus contractor, ~181 of 305 Oahu routes since 2014)
Multiple Honolulu private schools incl. Punahou, Iolani, Mid-Pacific, Maryknoll, Damien, Sacred Hearts, Saint Louis, UH Lab
recent_news: Roberts Hawaii has been the state's single largest school bus contractor since winning a $21M HIDOE contract in 2013; parent transitioned to 100% employee ownership via ESOP in 2008. The Honolulu Civil Beat 'Taken For A Ride' investigation (2011) detailed how Roberts came to dominate Hawaii school bus contracting.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Subsidiary of Roberts Hawaii Inc, a 1,400-employee, ~900-vehicle ESOP-owned tourism/transportation conglomerate that is itself the dominant statewide school bus operator. Strategic-scale, employee-owned, geographically isolated; would be a platform-level deal, not a regional bolt-on for Summit.

name: South Arkansas Bus Solutions
owner_or_parent: South Arkansas Business Solutions, Inc. (office products dealer, not a transportation contractor)
districts_or_customers: Office equipment / supply customers across south Arkansas; State of Arkansas vendor (office furniture/equipment)
recent_news: Despite the 'Bus Solutions' name in the source list, this is South Arkansas Business Solutions, Inc. - an office supply, equipment, and furniture dealer founded in July 2003, headquartered in Pine Bluff with the Rison address as a secondary location. Listed on Arkansas DFA vendor list and BBB as office furniture/contracting, not a school bus operator.
fit_notes: NOT a target - data error. Company is an office supply / furniture dealer (Bruce Mitchell, Pine Bluff/Rison), not a student transportation contractor. Exclude from Summit tuck-in pipeline.

name: E & M School Transportation
districts_or_customers: Collier Charter Academy and other Naples-area private/charter schools (Collier County)
recent_news: Operating ~12 years as E & M School Transportation Services of SW Florida, Inc. / E&M School Transportation Services of Naples; per third-party listings ~2 employees and ~$75K revenue at the primary site, suggesting a household-scale contractor running a handful of buses for Naples-area charter/private schools.
fit_notes: Confirmed independent Naples/Collier County family-owned charter-school transportation operator (Castillo family). Far below typical tuck-in size; relevant only as a SW Florida route add-on if Summit builds a Naples/Fort Myers footprint.
